data.title: OPEC Maintains U.S. Economic Growth Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
data.description: OPEC has released its monthly report, maintaining its forecast for U.S. economic growth at 2.2% for 2026 and 2% for 2027. According to Jin10, these projections reflect OPEC's assessment of the U.S. economic outlook over the next two years. The organization has not adjusted its expectations from previous reports, indicating a stable view of the U.S. economy's future performance. This forecast comes amid ongoing discussions about global economic trends and potential impacts on energy markets.

data.title: Strategy's STRC Preferred Stock Dividend and Short Interest Insights
data.description: Strategy's STRC preferred stock offers an annualized dividend of 11.5% on $5.3 billion outstanding. According to NS3.AI, this structure could incentivize hedge funds to increase short positions on MSTR. Currently, short interest surpasses 35 million MSTR shares, accounting for 11% of the float.

data.title: Strategy disclosed that it added 13,927 bitcoins last week.
data.description: According to market sources, Strategy disclosed that it increased its holdings of Bitcoin by 13,927 coins last week, worth approximately $1 billion.

data.title: Michael Saylor: Strategy's Bitcoin return year-to-date is 5.6%.
data.description: Michael Saylor stated in an article on the X platform that after purchasing 13,927 BTC, Strategy's total Bitcoin holdings reached 780,897, with a total acquisition cost of approximately $59.02 billion and a Bitcoin return of 5.6% since the beginning of 2026.

data.title: Pakistan, Egypt, and Türkiye will continue dialogue with the United States and Iran.
data.description: According to AXIOS: Pakistan, Egypt, and Türkiye will continue dialogue with the United States and Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: GFL to Acquire Secure Waste in C$6.4 Billion Deal
data.description: GFL Environmental Inc. has announced an agreement to acquire Secure Waste in a transaction valued at approximately C$6.4 billion ($4.6 billion). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this acquisition aims to expand GFL's presence in western Canada and enhance its services in the industrial and energy-linked waste sectors. This strategic move is expected to bolster GFL's market position and operational capabilities in these regions.

data.title: ECB Vice President Guindos: Rate Hike Depends on Secondary Effects
data.description: European Central Bank Vice President Luis de Guindos stated that any potential interest rate hikes will be contingent upon secondary effects. According to Jin10, Guindos emphasized the importance of monitoring these effects closely to determine the appropriate monetary policy actions. The ECB continues to assess economic indicators and market conditions to guide its decisions on interest rates.

data.title: The U.S. military will impose a blockade on the Arabian Sea and the Gulf of Oman east of the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A notice to crew members indicates that the U.S. military stated any vessels entering or leaving the blockade zone without authorization will be intercepted, diverted, and detained. The blockade applies to all shipping traffic, regardless of flag. The blockade will be implemented in the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Atomicals Market, a third-party Bitcoin marketplace, plans to cease operations on April 30.
data.description: Atomicals Market, a third-party Bitcoin marketplace, announced on the X platform that it plans to officially cease operations on April 30. Currently, all order and bid data are recorded off-chain and will no longer be displayed after the shutdown. Users do not need to perform any additional operations on order placement or pricing, but they need to transfer assets or export their AM Wallet private keys before the shutdown.

data.title: Israeli Prime Minister: Supports Trump's naval blockade against Iran
data.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu stated that Israel supports US President Donald Trump's decision to impose a naval blockade on Iran, adding that his government is maintaining full coordination with Washington on this matter. According to a video statement released by the Prime Minister's Office, Netanyahu said at a cabinet meeting: "Iran has violated the rules, and President Trump has decided to impose a naval blockade." "We certainly support this firm position, and we are maintaining continuous coordination with the United States." (Jin Shi)

data.title: US military: Strait of Hormuz to be closed; unauthorized vessels will not be allowed to pass.
data.description: The U.S. military announced that it will impose a blockade on the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea east of the Strait of Hormuz, and any vessels entering or leaving the blockade area without authorization may be intercepted, diverted, and detained. (Xinhua)

data.title: Superstate completes additional Series B funding round, with Invesco Private Capital, a subsidiary of Invesco, becoming a new investor.
data.description: Fintech company Superstate announced the completion of a Series B funding round, with Invesco Private Capital, a subsidiary of asset management giant Invesco, becoming a new investor. The amount was not disclosed. Simultaneously, Invesco will become the investment manager of Superstate's flagship tokenized fund, USTB (Short-Term US Treasury Bond Fund), starting in Q2 2026. This fund, with approximately $900 million in assets, is one of the world's largest tokenized government bond products. Following the handover, the fund will be renamed, and Superstate will retain its digital transfer agent functions. This move marks Invesco's full-scale entry into the blockchain fund sector. (Prnewswire)

data.title: ECB Vice President: Impact of Iran Conflict on Inflation Depends on Duration
data.description: The European Central Bank's Vice President, Luis de Guindos, has commented on the ongoing conflict involving Iran, stating that its impact on inflation will largely depend on how long the conflict persists. According to Jin10, Guindos emphasized that the duration of the conflict is a critical factor in determining its economic consequences. He noted that prolonged instability could exacerbate inflationary pressures, affecting the broader economy. The situation remains fluid, and the ECB is closely monitoring developments to assess potential impacts on monetary policy and economic stability.

data.title: Crypto Analyst Highlights RAVE Supply Concentration Concerns
data.description: Crypto analyst MLM has raised concerns about the concentration of RAVE token supply, noting that the Ravedao team effectively controls over 98% of the total supply. According to NS3.AI, a presumed team wallet holds 95.3% of the tokens, with additional suspected insider-held exchange wallets contributing to this concentration. MLM suggests that this level of control places RAVE's effective market capitalization at approximately $200 million. Meanwhile, CoinMarketCap lists the token at $9.47, with a fully diluted valuation of about $9.47 billion, following a significant seven-day increase of 3,747.59%.

data.title: Bitcoin Traders Brace for Price Decline Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description:
On April 13, Maxime Seiler, CEO of cryptocurrency trading firm STS Digital, highlighted that Bitcoin traders are preparing for a potential price drop. According to BlockBeats, Seiler's report indicates a significant demand for bearish options over bullish ones, with investors paying premiums for downside protection while selling bets on price increases, reflecting market anxiety.

This bearish sentiment emerges as Bitcoin trades slightly above $70,000. The cryptocurrency experienced a nearly 4% decline last weekend following U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to block the Hormuz Strait.

On Monday, the U.S. Central Command announced that the Navy would intercept all vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports starting at 10 a.m. Eastern Time. Consequently, oil prices surged above $100 per barrel, signaling increased inflationary pressures. Global central bank governors are expected to closely monitor these developments during their meeting in late April. The prices of risk assets like Bitcoin are heavily influenced by these institutions' policies, as they determine the monetary supply within the financial system. The unresolved Middle East conflict remains a key focus for the market this week.

data.title: Circle CEO Clarifies Stance on USDC Wallet Freezes Amid Criticism
data.description: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire addressed concerns regarding the company's policy on freezing USDC wallets, stating that Circle does not freeze wallets unless they are involved in legal proceedings. According to NS3.AI, this statement comes after criticism emerged over Circle's decision not to freeze $230 million in USDC linked to Drift. Earlier this month, Drift experienced a suspected cyberattack attributed to North Korean hackers, resulting in losses of up to $280 million.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin still has the potential to rise to $88,000, and the overall crypto market remains resilient.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently fluctuating around $70,000, with the overall crypto market showing resilience. Meanwhile, major crypto assets such as Ethereum, XRP, and Solana are also relatively stable. The key in the short term is whether Bitcoin can hold the important support level of $70,000. This level has become a watershed between bulls and bears and has been repeatedly defended by "bottom-fishing" funds. If it breaks down effectively, Bitcoin's price volatility could intensify due to the thin liquidity below. However, if it can stabilize, the market is expected to recover quickly. Some believe that with support from capital flows and macroeconomic factors, Bitcoin still has the potential to rise to $88,000. Overall, although Bitcoin's price performance is stable, speculative behavior combined with negative events may suppress market risk appetite in the short term. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitget's Q1 2026 Report Highlights Non-Crypto Asset Trading Volume
data.description: Bitget's Q1 2026 transparency report reveals that non-crypto assets, including commodities, constituted 20%–40% of the trading volume by the end of the first quarter. According to NS3.AI, crypto assets made up 60%–80% of the volume during the same period. The exchange also emphasized its UEX white paper and introduced new AI trading tools.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley Experience Pre-Market Declines
data.description: Goldman Sachs shares fell by 3.7% in pre-market trading, while Morgan Stanley's stock saw a 1.9% decline. According to Jin10, these movements come amid broader market fluctuations and investor concerns over potential economic impacts. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess the implications for the financial sector.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Consumption ETF Management Fee Reduction Announced
data.description: On April 13, Jin10 reported that the management fee annual rate for the Hong Kong Stock Consumption ETF managed by Yin Hua (159735) will be reduced from 0.50% to 0.15%. Additionally, the fund custody fee annual rate will be lowered from 0.10% to 0.05%. These changes will take effect starting April 14, 2026, inclusive.
